message.plain_text_body:
Hi there,

Thanks for reaching out about MarianneAI. We appreciate your message.

Our team is currently handling many public-data requests, so the automatic answer service is temporarily busy. Your request is captured and will be handled manually.

Current status: datagouv mcp failed: Datagouv MCP call failed: https://mcp.data.gouv.fr/mcp - HTTP status client error (406 Not Acceptable) for url (https://mcp.data.gouv.fr/mcp)

Please try again in a few minutes, and we will respond properly.

If this is urgent, resend with subject [urgent] and we will keep the ticket open.

Kind regards,
MarianneAI Support

classification.reasoning: The email contains a system error message detailing a failure with the Datagouv MCP integration (HTTP 406 Not Acceptable) when attempting to query French company public data, which qualifies as an operational problem or bug report.

intent.task_title: Resolve Datagouv MCP 406 Error for Company Data Query
intent.intent_summary: The user is attempting to retrieve public data about a French company, but their request failed due to an HTTP 406 Not Acceptable error from the Datagouv MCP integration.
intent.requested_outcome: Resolution of the system error so the user can successfully query and access public data about a company in France.
intent.task_description: Investigate and resolve the 'HTTP 406 Not Acceptable' error originating from the Datagouv MCP endpoint (https://mcp.data.gouv.fr/mcp). The error blocked the user's query regarding French company data. Fix the integration issue and reach out to the user to process their original data request.
missing_information: The specific name, SIREN, or SIRET of the company the user wants to inquire about
The exact request payload that triggered the 406 error

assessment.rationale: The user reported a specific integration bug (HTTP 406 error from Datagouv MCP endpoint) that occurred while trying to query French company data. This provides sufficient technical context to queue a debugging task.
missing_information: Specific company name, SIREN, or SIRET being queried
Exact request payload that triggered the 406 error
